Home / Project Management & Kanban / Alternatives to Jira

3 open source alternatives to Jira

Issue tracking and agile planning. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Jira

  • Overly complex for small teams.
  • Performance issues on large instances.
  • Atlassian's pricing and cloud-migration controversies.

Current Jira pricing (for reference): Free up to 10 users; Standard from $7.53/user/month.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Plane
Open source JIRA alternative with cycles and modules. 		Lightweight Jira alternative with similar modules and cycles. AGPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
OpenProject
Enterprise-ready open source project management. 		Regulated environments that need Jira's weight without the lock-in. GPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
Taiga
Agile project management with Scrum and Kanban. 		Scrum teams who want a focused agile tool. Mozilla-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes

1. Plane — Lightweight Jira alternative with similar modules and cycles.

Open source JIRA alternative with cycles and modules.

Strengths

  • Modern UI inspired by Linear/Jira.
  • Cycles, modules and views.
  • Active community and rapid updates.

Weaknesses

  • Still adding features at pace — occasional rough edges.
  • Self-hosted setup involves several services.
  • Fewer integrations than mature incumbents.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

Plane homepage · Source on GitHub · Jira vs Plane →

2. OpenProject — Regulated environments that need Jira's weight without the lock-in.

Enterprise-ready open source project management.

Strengths

  • Strong Gantt, roadmaps and budget features.
  • BIM (Building Information Modeling) for construction industry.
  • Active enterprise adoption.

Weaknesses

  • Community edition lacks some premium features.
  • Setup has more moving parts than lightweight tools.
  • UI density can intimidate newcomers.
License: GPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

OpenProject homepage · Source on GitHub · Jira vs OpenProject →

3. Taiga — Scrum teams who want a focused agile tool.

Agile project management with Scrum and Kanban.

Strengths

  • Dedicated Scrum and Kanban flows.
  • Clean, focused UI.
  • Free cloud tier available.

Weaknesses

  • Self-hosting is multi-service.
  • Smaller integration ecosystem than Jira.
  • Reporting features are lighter.
License: Mozilla-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

Taiga homepage · Source on GitHub · Jira vs Taiga →
